commands.author: yetval

target.title: fix(acpx): keep leaked non-openai model out of the Codex ACP thinking slot
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Spawning the Codex ACP agent fails during setup when the fleet's default/primary model belongs to a non-OpenAI provider. `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="codex")` resolves that default (for example `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`) into the Codex model slot, and the Codex ACP model parser then treats the segment after the `/` as a reasoning effort. The spawn aborts with:

```
spawn_failed: Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported.
Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh.
```

An explicit `thinking=low` does not help, because the model id is mis-routed before the thinking value is applied. Reported in #95780.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is in the Codex ACP model translation in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`. `normalizeCodexAcpModelOverride` split the ref on `/` and unconditionally treated the second segment as a reasoning effort:

```ts
// before
const parts = value.split("/");
...
const model = (parts[0] ?? "").trim();
const modelReasoningEffort = normalizeCodexAcpReasoningEffort(parts[1]);
```

For `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` this produced model `google` and reasoning effort `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, which `normalizeCodexAcpReasoningEffort` rejects, throwing before the explicit `thinking` override is consulted.

Codex is OpenAI-backed, so only an `openai/*` ref names a Codex model (AGENTS.md: "Runtime/setup/auth/catalog use openai + openai/*"). A non-OpenAI provider ref is a leaked default Codex cannot run: codex's `ReasoningEffort` is the closed set {none, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh} (codex-rs/protocol/src/openai_models.rs:40), and `-c model=` is a free string forwarded to the provider, so a non-OpenAI model id is never valid there.

The fix:
- Treat a trailing `/<segment>` as a reasoning-effort suffix only when that segment actually names one; otherwise it stays part of the model id, so a `provider/model` ref is never split into the reasoning-effort slot.
- A model id that is still provider-qualified after stripping `openai/` is a non-OpenAI model; drop it so Codex keeps its own default model, while still honoring an explicit `thinking` level.
- Route the Codex delegate model through `withCodexSessionModel` so a dropped model is also removed from the session input. Otherwise the raw `google/...` id still leaks to the acpx delegate via `sessionOptions.model`, where acpx's `applyRequestedModelIfAdvertised` throws `RequestedModelUnsupportedError` ("the ACP agent did not advertise that model"), simply moving the failure. This unifies Codex model handling into one path and removes the prior `normalizedInput` branch plus the now-dead reasoning-effort guard.

The change is confined to the Codex ACP owner boundary in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`. The Claude ACP sibling has no reasoning-effort slot, so it cannot misroute a model id into a thinking level; this issue (#95780) is specific to Codex.

## User Impact

Codex ACP spawns succeed again when the fleet default is a non-OpenAI model, falling back to Codex's own default model. An explicit `thinking` level is honored. Explicit `openai/*` model selections and `<model>/<reasoning-effort>` overrides are unchanged.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`: 50 passed (46 existing plus 4 new); the new cases fail on pristine main and pass with the patch.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` and `oxfmt --check` on the changed files: clean.
- tsgo core and core-test lanes: clean.
- End-to-end before/after against the real runtime: see Real behavior proof below.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex ACP spawn aborts when the fleet's non-OpenAI default model id is resolved into the Codex reasoning-effort slot, and an explicit thinking level does not help.
Real environment tested: the real AcpxRuntime.ensureSession class path on pristine main (2dc2d73b, with extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts identical to the captured base) and on the patched tree, driven with the codex agent registry. The only stub is the acpx delegate that spawns the codex-acp child process; the model parser, the Codex command translation, and the session-input handling all stay real.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: construct the real AcpxRuntime for agent "codex" and call ensureSession with model "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite" (first with no thinking, then with thinking "low"), plus an "openai/gpt-5.5" control.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main 2dc2d73b)
spawn codex, model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite (leaked fleet default, no thinking)
  -> THREW: Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh.
spawn codex, model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite, thinking=low
  -> THREW: Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported. Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh.
control: spawn codex, model=openai/gpt-5.5
  -> OK; delegate received model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}

# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs)
spawn codex, model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite (leaked fleet default, no thinking)
  -> OK; delegate received model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
spawn codex, model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite, thinking=low
  -> OK; delegate received model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
control: spawn codex, model=openai/gpt-5.5
  -> OK; delegate received model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}
```
Observed result after fix: the leaked non-OpenAI model id no longer aborts the Codex ACP spawn and never reaches the delegate; Codex starts on its own default model, the explicit low thinking level is preserved, and the openai/gpt-5.5 selection is unchanged.
What was not tested: no live codex-acp child process or live provider request; the acpx process-spawn delegate was stubbed. Full build not run.

Fixes #95780
